title: Pleioneura simulans
taxonomy: Pleioneura simulans is classified as a species within the genus Pleioneura, which belongs to the family Megaselidae. The genus Pleioneura is a group of marine snails, specifically sea slugs, that are known to be predators. The family Megaselidae is a part of the clade Heterobranchia, which consists of various species of slugs and snails. Within the clade Heterobranchia, the Megaselidae family is one of the more specific groups of marine gastropods. Further classification of Pleioneura simulans includes being a member of the phylum Mollusca, which encompasses a wide range of marine and freshwater invertebrates.

It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may provide relief from wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
